mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates a tightening labor market, increasing consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong aggregate demand, leading to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive 20d SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month rise in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% indicates inflationary pressures, prompting monetary tightening that reduces equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 115 signals robust manufacturing activity, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 115.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong labor market health, driving consumer confidence and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods with PAYEMS growth above 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in consumer credit (RSAFS) below 370 billion indicates reduced household leverage, lowering financial risk and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS falls below 370.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with a decline in capacity utilization (INDPRO) signals overheating supply chains, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of INDPRO > 115 and UNRATE < 4.7 fails to produce negative 20d SPX returns in > 50% of cases.
